questions.question: Explain the concept of Demand Paging. How does it differ from pure demand paging?
keyPoints: Demand paging loads pages only when referenced
Page fault occurs when requested page not in memory
OS brings page from secondary storage
Pure demand paging starts with no pages in memory
Difference: pure starts empty, demand can prepopulate

questions.question: What is Thrashing? Explain its causes and effects on system performance.
keyPoints: Thrashing is excessive paging activity
Caused by insufficient allocated frames
Page fault rate increases dramatically
CPU utilization decreases as OS busy swapping
Working set model can prevent thrashing

questions.question: Describe the differences between paging and segmentation.
keyPoints: Paging uses fixed-size blocks, segmentation variable-sized
Paging eliminates external fragmentation, segmentation does not
Segmentation provides logical view, paging provides physical
Page table vs segment table for address translation
Segmentation includes protection per segment

questions.question: Explain the purpose and functioning of a Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB).
keyPoints: TLB is hardware cache for address translations
Stores recent virtual to physical address mappings
Reduces memory access time for page table lookup
Hit ratio determines effectiveness
On TLB miss, page table is consulted and entry added to TLB

questions.question: What is the difference between internal and external fragmentation? Which memory management techniques suffer from each?
keyPoints: Internal: wasted memory inside allocated block due to fixed sizes
External: free memory scattered in non-contiguous blocks
Paging suffers from internal fragmentation
Contiguous allocation suffers from external fragmentation
Segmentation suffers from external fragmentation

questions.question: Explain the Copy-on-Write technique used in modern operating systems.
keyPoints: Parent and child processes share pages initially
Pages marked as read-only
Copy made only when process modifies page
Saves memory and processing time
Used in fork() system call

questions.question: Compare FIFO and LRU page replacement algorithms with respect to Belady's Anomaly.
keyPoints: FIFO can suffer from Belady's Anomaly
Adding more frames can increase page faults in FIFO
LRU does not suffer from Belady's Anomaly
More frames always improves or maintains performance in LRU
Belady's Anomaly proves optimal algorithm is not simple

questions.question: Explain the virtual memory concept in detail. Discuss how demand paging implements virtual memory and describe the steps involved in handling a page fault.
keyPoints: Virtual memory extends physical memory using disk
Gives illusion of larger memory space
Demand paging loads pages only when needed
Page fault handling: trap to OS, locate page on disk, find free frame, read page into frame, update page table, restart instruction
Benefits: larger programs, more processes, memory protection

questions.question: Explain the various page replacement algorithms. Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of FIFO, LRU, and Optimal algorithms.
keyPoints: FIFO: simple,可能出现Belady异常
LRU: good approximation of optimal,需要硬件支持
Optimal: 最小化页面故障,无法实际实现
FIFO优点:简单易实现,缺点:可能淘汰常用页面
LRU优点:效果好,缺点:实现开销大
Optimal作为理论基准

questions.question: Describe the structure of a page table and explain different types of page tables used in modern operating systems.
keyPoints: Page table maps virtual pages to physical frames
Each entry contains frame number and control bits
Types: hierarchical/multilevel, inverted, hashed page tables
Multilevel reduces memory overhead for large address spaces
Inverted page table has one entry per physical frame
Hashed page tables good for large address spaces

questions.question: Discuss memory management techniques in detail: Contiguous allocation, Paging, and Segmentation. Compare their advantages and disadvantages.
keyPoints: Contiguous: simple, but suffers from external fragmentation and size limitation
Paging: eliminates external fragmentation, suffers from internal fragmentation
Segmentation: logical division, provides protection, suffers from external fragmentation
Combined paging and segmentation offers advantages of both
Each technique has trade-offs between fragmentation, complexity, and performance
